query: You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.186 (commit: d6dd8b51). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: Can't display images with `#` in filenames ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? 1. Add a card including an image file containing `#` (hashtag) in its filename --- --- --- --- 3. Show the card in reviewer or previewer ### Expected behaviour The image is shown, as on 2.16.5. (2.16.5) ### Actual behaviour The broken-image icon is shown, and the image isn't shown. ### Debug info ```text AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.6 (568538896be943818ca793dd2635822265712def) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 11 (SDK 30) ProductFlavor = play Manufacturer = OPPO Model = CPH2013 Hardware = qcom Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 11; CPH2013 Build/RKQ1.200903.002; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/122.0.6261.119 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = df169a4d-29ec-46b6-b114-141ca7e01ba3 Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? - Originally reported in Anki Forum: https://forums.ankiweb.net/t/ankiweb-and-ankidroid-do-not-display-images-containing-pound-hashtag-sharp-symbol/42444/1 and dae has commented there. - As reported there, this issue occurs on AnkiWeb, too. ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/10436072/c588f585-b23c-4be6-b8f1-8a098a127705) So, strictly speaking, this is not a bug specific to AnkiDroid, but some approach specific to AnkiDroid may be needed, I'm not sure though. - This issue doesn't occur on Anki Desktop (as reported in Anki Forum) and on AnkiMobile. - The `#` is shown as `%23` on the editor of Anki Desktop (as reported in Anki Forum). ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/10436072/5ba75763-6007-4668-b036-0cb13e54cdc5) > \> Windows changes it back to a # in the sort field, but the HTML text remains unchanged > > This happens because Anki stores filenames without encoding on disk (legacy reasons), but needs to encode them before they’re shown in the editor. (dae) ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [X] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) (2.18alpha6) ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion It seems to have already been fixed in the current HEAD. I don't think so, it is ongoing discussion in the PR, https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/15909#issuecomment-2001940011 Thank you for the feedback. Not for objection (since I don't have enough knowledge about the topic), just for rewording more accurately and for what it is worth, the issue seems to have been fixed in my debug version build from a854e93 (Mar 17, 2024). On the other hand, a card with the image file added in Anki Desktop is not shown properly (i.e. shown as a broken-image icon) even in the debug version of AnkiDroid. In this case, `#` is shown as it is in the editor and the browser. “#” is a illegal char in HTML, so it’s not supposed to work anywhere if not encoded. Ankidroid encodes it when importing, and AFAIK Anki does it as well. > Ankidroid encodes it when importing In case that users import cards that contain image files with unencoded `#`, for example, importing the following shared deck, why images with `#` in filenames are shown in reviewer on 2.16.5, but not on 2.17.6? - https://ankiweb.net/shared/info/789434294 --- Read now damien's comment about "legacy reasons". Since `#` still isn't supposed to be treated as something else that isn't a URL fragment marker in that context, this is a deliberate anki breakage of the URL scheme. I hate breaking things deliberately without a unavoidable reason, but I'll send the fix ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `.idea/dictionaries/davidallison.xml` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 19→package com.ichi2.libanki

 21→import androidx.core.text.HtmlCompat
 22→import com.ichi2.libanki.Consts.FIELD_SEPARATOR
 23→import timber.log.Timber
 24→import java.io.*

 33→// TODO switch to standalone functions and properties and remove Utils container
 34→object Utils {
 35→    // Used to format doubles with English's decimal separator system
 36→    val ENGLISH_LOCALE = Locale("en_US")
 37→
 38→    // Regex pattern used in removing tags from text before diff
 39→    private val commentPattern = Pattern.compile("(?s)<!--.*?-->")
 40→    private val stylePattern = Pattern.compile("(?si)<style.*?>.*?</style>")
 41→    private val scriptPattern = Pattern.compile("(?si)<script.*?>.*?</script>")
 42→    private val tagPattern = Pattern.compile("(?s)<.*?>")
 43→    private val imgPattern = Pattern.compile("(?i)<img[^>]+src=[\"']?([^\"'>]+)[\"']?[^>]*>")
 44→    private val typePattern = Pattern.compile("(?s)\\[\\[type:.+?]]")
 45→    private val avRefPattern = Pattern.compile("(?s)\\[anki:play:.:\\d+?]")
 46→    private val htmlEntitiesPattern = Pattern.compile("&#?\\w+;")
 47→    private const val ALL_CHARACTERS =
 48→        "0123456789ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz"
 49→    private const val BASE91_EXTRA_CHARS = "!#$%&()*+,-./:;<=>?@[]^_`{|}~"
 50→
 51→    /*
 52→     * Locale
 53→     * ***********************************************************************************************
 54→     */
 55→    /*
 56→    * HTML
 57→     * ***********************************************************************************************
 58→     */
 59→    /**
 60→     * Strips a text from <style>...</style>, <script>...</script> and <_any_tag_> HTML tags.
 61→     * @param inputParam The HTML text to be cleaned.
 62→     * @return The text without the aforementioned tags.
 63→     </_any_tag_> */
 64→    fun stripHTML(inputParam: String): String {
 65→        var s = commentPattern.matcher(inputParam).replaceAll("")
 66→        s = stripHTMLScriptAndStyleTags(s)
 67→        s = tagPattern.matcher(s).replaceAll("")
 68→        return entsToTxt(s)
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    /**
 72→     * Strips <style>...</style> and <script>...</script> HTML tags and content from a string.
 73→     * @param inputParam The HTML text to be cleaned.
 74→     * @return The text without the aforementioned tags.
 75→     */
 76→    fun stripHTMLScriptAndStyleTags(inputParam: String): String {
 77→        var htmlMatcher = stylePattern.matcher(inputParam)
 78→        val s = htmlMatcher.replaceAll("")
 79→        htmlMatcher = scriptPattern.matcher(s)
 80→        return htmlMatcher.replaceAll("")
 81→    }
 82→
 83→    /**
 84→     * Strip HTML but keep media filenames
 85→     */
 86→    fun stripHTMLMedia(s: String, replacement: String = " $1 "): String {
 87→        val imgMatcher = imgPattern.matcher(s)
 88→        return stripHTML(imgMatcher.replaceAll(replacement))
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    /**
 92→     * Takes a string and replaces all the HTML symbols in it with their unescaped representation.
 93→     * This should only affect substrings of the form `&something;` and not tags.
 94→     * Internet rumour says that Html.fromHtml() doesn't cover all cases, but it doesn't get less
 95→     * vague than that.
 96→     * @param htmlInput The HTML escaped text
 97→     * @return The text with its HTML entities unescaped.
 98→     */
 99→    // TODO see if method can be refactored to remove the reference to HtmlCompat
100→    private fun entsToTxt(htmlInput: String): String {
101→        // entitydefs defines nbsp as \xa0 instead of a standard space, so we
102→        // replace it first
103→        val html = htmlInput.replace(" ", " ")
104→        val htmlEntities = htmlEntitiesPattern.matcher(html)
105→        val sb = StringBuffer()
106→        while (htmlEntities.find()) {
107→            val spanned =
108→                HtmlCompat.fromHtml(htmlEntities.group(), HtmlCompat.FROM_HTML_MODE_LEGACY)
109→            val replacement = Matcher.quoteReplacement(spanned.toString())
110→            htmlEntities.appendReplacement(sb, replacement)
111→        }
112→        htmlEntities.appendTail(sb)
113→        return sb.toString()
114→    }
115→
116→    /**
117→     * Strip special fields like `[[type:...]]` and `[anki:play...]` from a string.
118→     * @param input The text to be cleaned.
119→     * @return The text without special fields.
120→     */
121→    fun stripSpecialFields(input: String): String {
122→        val s = typePattern.matcher(input).replaceAll("")
123→        return avRefPattern.matcher(s).replaceAll("")
124→    }
125→
126→    /**
127→     * Strip HTML and special fields from a string.
128→     * @param input The text to be cleaned.
129→     * @return The text without HTML and special fields.
130→     */
131→    fun stripHTMLAndSpecialFields(input: String): String {
132→        val s = stripHTML(input)
133→        return stripSpecialFields(s)
134→    }
135→
136→    /*
137→     * IDs
138→     * ***********************************************************************************************
139→     */
140→    /** Given a list of integers, return a string '(int1,int2,...)'.  */
141→    fun ids2str(ids: IntArray?): String = StringBuilder().apply {
142→        append("(")
143→        if (ids != null) {
144→            val s = Arrays.toString(ids)
145→            append(s.substring(1, s.length - 1))
146→        }
147→        append(")")
148→    }.toString()
149→
150→    /** Given a list of integers, return a string '(int1,int2,...)'.  */
151→    fun ids2str(ids: LongArray?): String = StringBuilder().apply {
152→        append("(")
153→        if (ids != null) {
154→            val s = Arrays.toString(ids)
155→            append(s.substring(1, s.length - 1))
156→        }
157→        append(")")
158→    }.toString()
159→
160→    /** Given a list of integers, return a string '(int1,int2,...)', in order given by the iterator.  */
161→    fun <T> ids2str(ids: Iterable<T>): String = StringBuilder(512).apply {
162→        append("(")
163→        for ((index, id) in ids.withIndex()) {
164→            if (index != 0) {
165→                append(", ")
166→            }
167→            append(id)
168→        }
169→        append(")")
170→    }.toString()
171→
172→    // used in ankiweb
173→    private fun base62(numParam: Int, @Suppress("SameParameterValue") extra: String): String {
174→        var num = numParam
175→        val table = ALL_CHARACTERS + extra
176→        val len = table.length
177→        var buf = ""
178→        var mod: Int
179→        while (num != 0) {
180→            mod = num % len
181→            buf += table.substring(mod, mod + 1)
182→            num /= len
183→        }
184→        return buf
185→    }
186→
187→    // all printable characters minus quotes, backslash and separators
188→    private fun base91(num: Int): String {
189→        return base62(num, BASE91_EXTRA_CHARS)
190→    }
191→
192→    /** return a base91-encoded 64bit random number  */
193→    fun guid64(): String {
194→        return base91(
195→            Random().nextInt((2.0.pow(61.0) - 1).toInt())
196→        )
197→    }
198→
199→    /**
200→     * Fields
201→     * ***********************************************************************************************
202→     */
203→    fun joinFields(list: Array<String>): String {
204→        val result = StringBuilder(128)
205→        for (i in 0 until list.size - 1) {
206→            result.append(list[i]).append("\u001f")
207→        }
208→        if (list.isNotEmpty()) {
209→            result.append(list[list.size - 1])
210→        }
211→        return result.toString()
212→    }
213→
214→    // TODO ensure manual conversion is correct
215→    fun splitFields(fields: String): MutableList<String> {
216→        // -1 ensures that we don't drop empty fields at the ends
217→        return fields.split(FIELD_SEPARATOR).toMutableList()
218→    }
219→
220→    /*
221→     * Checksums
222→     * ***********************************************************************************************
223→     */
224→    /**
225→     * SHA1 checksum.
226→     * Equivalent to python sha1.hexdigest()
227→     *
228→     * @param data the string to generate hash from
229→     * @return A string of length 40 containing the hexadecimal representation of the MD5 checksum of data.
230→     */
231→    fun checksum(data: String?): String {
232→        if (data == null) {
233→            return ""
234→        }
235→        val md: MessageDigest
236→        var digest: ByteArray? = null
237→        try {
238→            md = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA1")
239→            digest = md.digest(data.toByteArray(charset("UTF-8")))
240→        } catch (e: NoSuchAlgorithmException) {
241→            Timber.e(e, "Utils.checksum: No such algorithm.")
242→            throw RuntimeException(e)
243→        } catch (e: UnsupportedEncodingException) {
244→            Timber.e(e, "Utils.checksum :: UnsupportedEncodingException")
245→        }
246→        val biginteger = BigInteger(1, digest)
247→        var result = biginteger.toString(16)
248→
249→        // pad with zeros to length of 40 This method used to pad
250→        // to the length of 32. As it turns out, sha1 has a digest
251→        // size of 160 bits, leading to a hex digest size of 40,
252→        // not 32.
253→        if (result.length < 40) {
254→            val zeroes = "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000"
255→            result = zeroes.substring(0, zeroes.length - result.length) + result
256→        }
257→        return result
258→    }
259→
260→    /**
261→     * Optimized in case of sortIdx = 0
262→     * @param fields Fields of a note
263→     * @param sortIdx An index of the field
264→     * @return The field at sortIdx, without html media, and the csum of the first field.
265→     */
266→    fun sfieldAndCsum(fields: List<String>, sortIdx: Int): Pair<String, Long> {
267→        val firstStripped = stripHTMLMedia(fields[0])
268→        val sortStripped = if (sortIdx == 0) firstStripped else stripHTMLMedia(fields[sortIdx])
269→        return Pair(sortStripped, fieldChecksumWithoutHtmlMedia(firstStripped))
270→    }
271→
272→    /**
273→     * @param data the string to generate hash from. Html media should be removed
274→     * @return 32 bit unsigned number from first 8 digits of sha1 hash
275→     */
276→    private fun fieldChecksumWithoutHtmlMedia(data: String?): Long {
277→        return java.lang.Long.valueOf(checksum(data).substring(0, 8), 16)
278→    }
279→}

 59→    /**
 60→     * Strips a text from <style>...</style>, <script>...</script> and <_any_tag_> HTML tags.
 61→     * @param inputParam The HTML text to be cleaned.
 62→     * @return The text without the aforementioned tags.
 63→     </_any_tag_> */
 64→    fun stripHTML(inputParam: String): String {
 65→        var s = commentPattern.matcher(inputParam).replaceAll("")
 66→        s = stripHTMLScriptAndStyleTags(s)
 67→        s = tagPattern.matcher(s).replaceAll("")
 68→        return entsToTxt(s)
 69→    }

 76→    fun stripHTMLScriptAndStyleTags(inputParam: String): String {
 77→        var htmlMatcher = stylePattern.matcher(inputParam)
 78→        val s = htmlMatcher.replaceAll("")
 79→        htmlMatcher = scriptPattern.matcher(s)
 80→        return htmlMatcher.replaceAll("")
 81→    }

 91→    /**
 92→     * Takes a string and replaces all the HTML symbols in it with their unescaped representation.
 93→     * This should only affect substrings of the form `&something;` and not tags.
 94→     * Internet rumour says that Html.fromHtml() doesn't cover all cases, but it doesn't get less
 95→     * vague than that.
 96→     * @param htmlInput The HTML escaped text
 97→     * @return The text with its HTML entities unescaped.
 98→     */
 99→    // TODO see if method can be refactored to remove the reference to HtmlCompat
100→    private fun entsToTxt(htmlInput: String): String {
101→        // entitydefs defines nbsp as \xa0 instead of a standard space, so we
102→        // replace it first
103→        val html = htmlInput.replace(" ", " ")
104→        val htmlEntities = htmlEntitiesPattern.matcher(html)
105→        val sb = StringBuffer()
106→        while (htmlEntities.find()) {
107→            val spanned =
108→                HtmlCompat.fromHtml(htmlEntities.group(), HtmlCompat.FROM_HTML_MODE_LEGACY)
109→            val replacement = Matcher.quoteReplacement(spanned.toString())
110→            htmlEntities.appendReplacement(sb, replacement)
111→        }
112→        htmlEntities.appendTail(sb)
113→        return sb.toString()
114→    }

121→    fun stripSpecialFields(input: String): String {
122→        val s = typePattern.matcher(input).replaceAll("")
123→        return avRefPattern.matcher(s).replaceAll("")

131→    fun stripHTMLAndSpecialFields(input: String): String {
132→        val s = stripHTML(input)
133→        return stripSpecialFields(s)

172→    // used in ankiweb
173→    private fun base62(numParam: Int, @Suppress("SameParameterValue") extra: String): String {
174→        var num = numParam
175→        val table = ALL_CHARACTERS + extra
176→        val len = table.length
177→        var buf = ""
178→        var mod: Int
179→        while (num != 0) {
180→            mod = num % len
181→            buf += table.substring(mod, mod + 1)
182→            num /= len
183→        }
184→        return buf
185→    }

187→    // all printable characters minus quotes, backslash and separators
188→    private fun base91(num: Int): String {
189→        return base62(num, BASE91_EXTRA_CHARS)
190→    }

193→    fun guid64(): String {
194→        return base91(
195→            Random().nextInt((2.0.pow(61.0) - 1).toInt())
196→        )

203→    fun joinFields(list: Array<String>): String {
204→        val result = StringBuilder(128)
205→        for (i in 0 until list.size - 1) {
206→            result.append(list[i]).append("\u001f")
207→        }
208→        if (list.isNotEmpty()) {
209→            result.append(list[list.size - 1])
210→        }
211→        return result.toString()

226→     * Equivalent to python sha1.hexdigest()
227→     *
228→     * @param data the string to generate hash from
229→     * @return A string of length 40 containing the hexadecimal representation of the MD5 checksum of data.
230→     */
231→    fun checksum(data: String?): String {
232→        if (data == null) {
233→            return ""
234→        }
235→        val md: MessageDigest
236→        var digest: ByteArray? = null
237→        try {
238→            md = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA1")
239→            digest = md.digest(data.toByteArray(charset("UTF-8")))
240→        } catch (e: NoSuchAlgorithmException) {
241→            Timber.e(e, "Utils.checksum: No such algorithm.")
242→            throw RuntimeException(e)
243→        } catch (e: UnsupportedEncodingException) {
244→            Timber.e(e, "Utils.checksum :: UnsupportedEncodingException")
245→        }
246→        val biginteger = BigInteger(1, digest)
247→        var result = biginteger.toString(16)
248→
249→        // pad with zeros to length of 40 This method used to pad
250→        // to the length of 32. As it turns out, sha1 has a digest
251→        // size of 160 bits, leading to a hex digest size of 40,
252→        // not 32.
253→        if (result.length < 40) {
254→            val zeroes = "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000"
255→            result = zeroes.substring(0, zeroes.length - result.length) + result
256→        }
257→        return result
258→    }
259→
260→    /**
261→     * Optimized in case of sortIdx = 0
262→     * @param fields Fields of a note
263→     * @param sortIdx An index of the field
264→     * @return The field at sortIdx, without html media, and the csum of the first field.
265→     */
266→    fun sfieldAndCsum(fields: List<String>, sortIdx: Int): Pair<String, Long> {
267→        val firstStripped = stripHTMLMedia(fields[0])
268→        val sortStripped = if (sortIdx == 0) firstStripped else stripHTMLMedia(fields[sortIdx])
269→        return Pair(sortStripped, fieldChecksumWithoutHtmlMedia(firstStripped))
270→    }
271→
272→    /**
273→     * @param data the string to generate hash from. Html media should be removed
274→     * @return 32 bit unsigned number from first 8 digits of sha1 hash
275→     */
276→    private fun fieldChecksumWithoutHtmlMedia(data: String?): Long {
277→        return java.lang.Long.valueOf(checksum(data).substring(0, 8), 16)
278→    }
279→}
